title: Bogotá 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Bogotá 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Bogotá, Colombia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Museo del Oro (Gold Museum) and Monserrate. Prices from $1248 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Bogotá, Colombia — Mild, spring-like climate year-round thanks to its 2,640m altitude — daytime highs rarely stray from the high teens Celsius, with frequent afternoon showers regardless of season. December to March and July to August — the driest months, though Bogotá's high altitude keeps it cool and spring-like nearly year-round. This itinerary blends Museo del Oro (Gold Museum), Monserrate, La Candelaria with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Museo del Oro (Gold Museum), Monserrate, La Candelaria
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Bogotá
itinerary.description: Land at El Dorado International Airport (BOG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Museo del Oro (Gold Museum) — World's largest collection of pre-Hispanic gold artifacts. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Bogotá: Monserrate & Catedral de Sal de Zipaquirá
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Monserrate — Mountaintop church reached by cable car or funicular, panoramic city views. In the afternoon, continue to Catedral de Sal de Zipaquirá — Underground salt-mine cathedral, a popular day trip. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bogotá: Bogotá graffiti walking tour & Casa de Moneda
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Bogotá graffiti walking tour — Guided street-art tour through La Candelaria. In the afternoon, continue to Casa de Moneda — Colonial-era mint building and coin history museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Zipaquirá Salt Cathedral
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Zipaquirá Salt Cathedral, a popular side trip from Bogotá. Return to Bogotá in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Zipaquirá Salt Cathedral

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Jardín Botánico de Bogotá — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Bogotá: Quinta de Bolívar & La Candelaria
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Quinta de Bolívar — Restored colonial house museum once home to Simón Bolívar. In the afternoon, continue to La Candelaria — Colorful colonial-era historic center with cobblestone streets. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Guatavita Lagoon
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Guatavita Lagoon, a popular side trip from Bogotá. Return to Bogotá in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Bogotá: Museo Botero & Plaza de Bolívar
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Museo Botero — Free museum of Fernando Botero's distinctive rotund figures. In the afternoon, continue to Plaza de Bolívar — The city's main square, ringed by government and cathedral buildings. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Quebrada de la Vieja nature trail — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Quebrada de la Vieja nature trail

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Villa de Leyva colonial town
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Villa de Leyva colonial town, a popular side trip from Bogotá. Return to Bogotá in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Villa de Leyva colonial town

itinerary.title: Bogotá: Chorro de Quevedo & Parque de la Independencia
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Chorro de Quevedo — Small plaza said to mark the city's founding spot. In the afternoon, continue to Parque de la Independencia — Central city park near the bullring and planetarium. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bogotá: Museo del Oro (Gold Museum) & Monserrate
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Museo del Oro (Gold Museum) — World's largest collection of pre-Hispanic gold artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Monserrate — Mountaintop church reached by cable car or funicular, panoramic city views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Paloquemao Market — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Catedral de Sal de Zipaquirá or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to BOG for your departure flight.

travelTips: Take it easy on arrival — the 2,640m altitude can cause mild breathlessness for the first day or two.
Use rideshare apps rather than hailing street taxis, especially at night.
Sundays bring the Ciclovía, when major avenues close to cars for cyclists and runners — a great free activity.
Carry only the cash you need and keep phones out of sight in crowded areas of downtown.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Layers — mornings and evenings are cool at altitude even though days feel mild
A light rain jacket, since afternoon showers are frequent
Comfortable walking shoes for La Candelaria's cobblestones
thingsToDo: Paragliding over the Sabana de Bogotá
Cycling the Sunday Ciclovía street closures
thingsToAvoid: Hailing an unmetered street taxi rather than booking through an app.
Walking alone in La Candelaria or downtown late at night.
Flashing expensive jewelry, watches, or phones in crowded public areas.
topRestaurants: Andrés Carne de Res — Colombian grill, dinner theater ($$$)
Leo Cocina y Cava — Amazonian-Colombian fine dining ($$$$)
La Puerta Falsa — Traditional Colombian, tamales and ajiaco ($)
hiddenGems: Quebrada de la Vieja nature trail
adventureActivities: Paragliding over the Sabana de Bogotá
Cycling the Sunday Ciclovía street closures
luxuryExperiences: Private Zipaquirá salt cathedral tour with driver
Tasting menu at Leo Cocina y Cava
Suite stay at a restored colonial hotel in La Candelaria
honeymoonExperiences: Private dinner in a La Candelaria courtyard restaurant
Day trip to the Zipaquirá Salt Cathedral
budgetExperiences: Free Sunday Ciclovía when major roads close to cyclists
Wandering La Candelaria's free street art
localEtiquette: Greet with a handshake on first meeting; close friends may greet with a single cheek kiss.
Use the formal "usted" with strangers, service staff, and elders until invited to switch to "tú".
Dress is generally smart-casual — Bogotanos dress a notch more formally than in beach cities.
Punctuality is loosely observed socially but expected for business meetings.
localTransportGuide: The TransMilenio bus rapid transit system covers most of the city on dedicated lanes and is the cheapest way to get around, though it gets very crowded at rush hour. Rideshare apps (Uber, Didi) are widely used and generally more comfortable for visitors; taxis should be booked via app rather than hailed on the street.
bestTimeToVisit: December to March and July to August — the driest months, though Bogotá's high altitude keeps it cool and spring-like nearly year-round.
weather: Mild, spring-like climate year-round thanks to its 2,640m altitude — daytime highs rarely stray from the high teens Celsius, with frequent afternoon showers regardless of season.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need a Colombian e-visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~17.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Bogotá?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need a Colombian e-visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Bogotá?
faqs.answer: December to March and July to August — the driest months, though Bogotá's high altitude keeps it cool and spring-like nearly year-round. Mild, spring-like climate year-round thanks to its 2,640m altitude — daytime highs rarely stray from the high teens Celsius, with frequent afternoon showers regardless of season.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Bogotá safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Bogotá is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Bogotá?
faqs.answer: The local currency is COP. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Isabella Tanaka
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Chorro de Quevedo and Chorro de Quevedo. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Arjun van Dijk
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Museo Botero in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: David Rossi
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Bogotá is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Quinta de Bolívar. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Grace Smith
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Museo del Oro (Gold Museum) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Priya Novak
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Bogotá graffiti walking tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Tom Sharma
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Monserrate and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mohammed Kim
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Parque de la Independencia alone was worth the trip, but combined with Museo del Oro (Gold Museum) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sofia Nguyen
